claim_boundary: finite theorem/no-go audit plus sharpened missing assumption; not a continuum de Sitter theorem, dS/CFT construction, or ER=EPR proof

major_claims.claim: Five anti-tautological subsystem candidates are audited: low angular modes, fuzzy coordinate polynomials, coherent localized patches, matrix-valued Toeplitz fibers, and heat-stable low-energy systems.
Canonical scalar fuzzy-sphere routes can preserve screen data and norms while their commutator scale vanishes in the large-cutoff limit.
Screen-shadow preservation and norm faithfulness do not imply persistent noncommutative response.
Coherent patches, matrix-valued Toeplitz fibers, and heat-stable low-energy routes can support the desired theorem only after a canonical noncommutative selection principle is supplied.
Heat stability and strong continuity are not enough unless the selected low-energy sector contains a fixed noncommutative subsystem.
The minimal missing assumption is sharpened to a canonical noncommutative subsystem with nonzero large-cutoff commutator lower bound.

expert_question: Does static-patch/fuzzy-sphere physics supply a canonical noncommutative matrix fiber, coherent patch, or heat-stable operator subsystem with cutoff-independent commutator scale, or must the observer algebra be added as extra non-screen data?
